claim_text: IEA says that by 2035 China is set to supply around 80% of refined magnet rare earth elements.
notes: Direct support for durable refining concentration.

claim_text: IEA says China accounted for about 91% of magnet rare earth separation and refining production in 2024.
notes: Supports concentration at the refining/separation layer.

claim_text: IEA says China accounts for 94% of sintered permanent magnet production used across cars, wind turbines, industrial motors, data centres, and defence systems.
notes: Strong support that finished magnet manufacturing is the most extreme bottleneck layer.

claim_text: China imposed export controls on certain medium and heavy rare earth items on April 4, 2025.
IEA says export volumes fell sharply after the April 2025 controls and some manufacturers had to cut utilization or temporarily shut factories.
notes: Shows the concentration risk already translated into real industrial disruption.

claim_text: DoD says terbium is essential for NdFeB magnets in key defense systems and funded domestic production expansion.
notes: Supports heavy rare earths as a distinct bottleneck.

claim_text: DoD funded domestic rare earth permanent magnet manufacturing through E-VAC/VAC to strengthen U.S. supply chains.
notes: Supports magnet manufacturing as a strategic and scarce layer.

claim_text: MP Materials restored U.S. rare earth magnet production in 2025 and began trial production of automotive-grade sintered NdFeB magnets in Texas.
notes: Supports live domestic diversification at the magnet layer.

claim_text: MP Materials' July 2025 DoD partnership added a long-term NdPr price floor, heavy rare earth separation expansion, and a second magnet facility.
notes: Supports mine-to-magnet expansion and policy-backed economics.

claim_text: Lynas is the largest rare earths producer outside China and has U.S. heavy rare earth separation buildout backed by DoD.
notes: Home page supports its non-China significance; DoD-backed HRE facility adds strategic relevance.

claim_text: Lynas was awarded a U.S. DoD contract to build a commercial heavy rare earth facility in Texas.
notes: Supports allied heavy rare earth diversification.

claim_text: Rare earth mining alone is the dominant bottleneck in this chain.
source_type: inference contradicted by stronger midstream/downstream concentration
notes: Evidence points more strongly to refining and magnet manufacturing.

claim_text: Non-China diversification has already solved the rare-earth bottleneck problem.
source_type: inference contradicted by state-backed expansion and IEA concentration data
notes: Diversification is underway but still far from removing concentration risk.
